results.identified_issue:
Three separate defects, one per ask.

(1) Forward skipping. There was no skip-forward BUTTON, but the scrubber was draggable and capped at farthestProgress — a high-water mark that never regresses. So after skipping back 15s you could drag forward again over ground you'd already covered. That is skip-forward in everything but name. Rather than tighten the cap I removed the whole affordance: the bar is now PlaybackIndicator (no thumb, no gesture, allowsHitTesting(false)), and ClipPreviewPlayer.seek clamps to min(seconds, progress), so forward seeking is impossible at the model layer even if a future view re-adds a drag. farthestProgress is deleted — with no forward seek it was redundant, and it was itself one of the 'third internal states'. showSkipForward/onSkipForward are deleted from NowPlayingContent so the button cannot be reintroduced by passing a flag.

(2) Controls out of sync. isPlaying was a stored Bool written from ~10 sites, with a userPaused flag that FORCED it false even when the engine was playing, and natural end faked userPaused = true just to stop play() from restarting the clip. TTS progress was a wall-clock Timer, not the synthesizer. Now: syncPlaybackStateFromEngine() is the only writer of isPlaying and it READS the engine (AVPlayer.timeControlStatus != .paused, or synthesizer.isSpeaking && !isPaused); it's called from the timeControlStatus KVO and every AVSpeechSynthesizerDelegate callback. userPaused survives as pausedByUser but is intent only — it gates auto-resume in play(), never display. Natural end no longer lies: play() guards on hasFullyHeardCurrent instead. TTS progress now comes from willSpeakRangeOfSpeechString (real spoken character range / total characters), so the bar tracks actual speech. That also fixes a real bug: the wall clock could hit the estimated duration BEFORE the speech finished, marking the clip fully-heard and unlocking the next card early.

Also replaced the hasFullyHeardCurrent -> 'insert currentClip?.id' pattern with a published finishedClipID, so a card change racing with completion can't credit the wrong clip.

(3) The lock. The next clip wasn't merely locked, it was absent — scrollableClips was prefix(maxAllowedIndex + 1), so there was physically nothing below to scroll to. Now it's prefix(maxAllowedIndex + 2): one peek page is rendered, frosted with a lock icon, and landing on it triggers bounceBackToCurrent() which animates currentID back and shows a transient banner.

Assumptions / not verified: TTS duration is still an ESTIMATE (ArgumentDeckBuilder guesses ~2.3 words/sec) — AVSpeechSynthesizer can't report a true length ahead of time. Progress is now honest as a fraction of the answer, and the bar lands exactly on the end at the last word, but the mm:ss labels are approximate for text-only answers. Also note every clip is text-only today (SubmitAnswerView always saves audioFileName: nil), so the AVPlayer path is currently unexercised; I kept it correct but only the TTS path will actually run on device. One deliberate behavior choice: skipping back during TTS resumes speech, because a synthesizer can't seek — it has to re-speak from a word boundary. I set pausedByUser = false there so isPlaying stays truthful rather than claiming paused while talking. Typechecked the full app against the iOS SDK (swiftc -typecheck, clean); not run on device.
results.summary: Made the bar a read-only playback indicator, wired every control to real engine state, and added a peek-then-bounce lock on the next item.
results.detailed_description:
All three things you asked for are in.

Skip-forward is gone. There was no forward button, but the bar itself was draggable — and because its cap was the furthest point you'd ever reached (not where you are now), skipping back 15 seconds let you drag forward again. That was the skip-forward you were seeing. The bar is now a pure playback indicator: no thumb, no drag, it doesn't take touches at all. I also made it impossible underneath the UI — the player's seek() clamps to your current position, so nothing can move you forward except listening. Skip-back still works.

The controls now read the engine instead of a copy of it. The play/pause button was driven by a stored flag that ten different places wrote to, plus a 'userPaused' override that could force it to say paused while audio was actually playing — and when a clip ended naturally the code faked a user-pause just to stop it restarting. All of that is gone: the button now reflects AVPlayer's timeControlStatus, or the speech synthesizer's own isSpeaking/isPaused, and nothing else. The progress bar for spoken answers used to be a stopwatch guessing where the voice was; it now follows the synthesizer's actual spoken position. That fixed a real bug you hadn't hit yet — the stopwatch could reach the estimated end before the voice finished, marking the clip heard and unlocking the next one early.

Scroll-down now works the way you described. Previously the next item wasn't locked so much as nonexistent — it wasn't in the scroll list, so there was nothing below you. Now the next viewpoint is rendered as a frosted, dimmed page with a lock icon. You can scroll down and see it, then the feed animates you back up and shows a banner: 'Finish listening to this one first.' Its controls are dead while locked. Once you finish the current clip it unfrosts and you can move on.

One thing to know: every answer in the deck today is text-only, so playback runs through the speech synthesizer, and a synthesizer can't tell us a clip's true length in advance. The bar position is now honest (it lands on the end exactly at the last word), but the mm:ss numbers are still an estimate for those. Related: skipping back during spoken playback resumes the voice, because a synthesizer can't seek — it has to re-speak from a word boundary. Say the word if either of those bothers you.

I typechecked the whole app against the iOS SDK and it's clean; I haven't run it on the device.

description: Playback doesn't seem to work correctly. One, I can skip forward. That shouldn't be there. The bar should just be a playback indicator. No ability to skip forward. We do have the ability to skip backwards though. And then, make sure all the controls are synced exactly with the actual state of playback. Not some third internal app state. uh secondly we should be if we scroll down we should be able to see the next thing the next item but then it should bounce us back up returning a lock icon saying you have to finish listening to this one first
